source: Left 4 Dead 2_Scripting_Game Events - Valve Developer Community.html

events.name: player_death
events.description: a game event, name may be 32 characters long; this extends the original player_death by a new field

arguments.name: long
arguments.description: entity ID who died, userid should be used first, to get the dead Player. Otherwise, it is not a player, so use this.

arguments.name: string
arguments.description: What type of zombie, so we don't have zombie names

arguments.name: long
arguments.description: if the killer is not a player, the entindex of who killed. Again, use the attacker first

arguments.name: string
arguments.description: What type of zombie, so we don't have zombie names

events.name: player_hurt
events.description: Registers all playable classes (Hunter, Smoker, Boomer, Tank, Survivors). See infected_hurt for Witch and Common Infected
notes: Fires when a player entity takes damage.
Does not fire when a survivor takes enough damage to become incapacitated.

arguments.name: long
arguments.description: entity id who attacked, if attacker not a player, and userid therefore invalid

arguments.name: string
arguments.description: weapon name attacker used, if not the world

events.name: player_activate
events.description: The earliest game event that generates when a CTerrorPlayer entity is being spawned

events.name: player_team
notes: Fires when a player switches team.

events.name: player_bot_replace
notes: Fires when a bot replaces a player. Related Events: Fires alongside player_first_spawn in order to spawn the bot; this event fires afterwards.

events.name: bot_player_replace
notes: Fires when a player replaces a bot.
Imagine using the slot operator when reading the name of these events bot <- player to remember whether the bot is being replaced or a player

events.name: weapon_fire
events.description: For example: interrupting a Molotov throw by switching right before it's about to be thrown.
notes: It fires before firing the weapon, even if it's being interrupted. For example: interrupting a Molotov throw by switching right before it's about to be thrown.

arguments.description: don't network this, its way too spammy

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: user ID of the player who fires their weapon
don't network this, its way too spammy

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: user ID of the player who fires their weapon

events.name: weapon_reload
notes: Fires when a survivor reloads their weapon.

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: user ID of the player who reloads

events.name: weapon_zoom
notes: Fires when a survivor uses the zoom feature on snipers.
It fires when pressing the zoom button while holding a sniper, instead of zooming the weapon.

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: user ID of the survivor who zooms their weapon

events.name: ability_use
notes: Fires when an infected uses their ability Issues: Doesn't fire for jockey

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: user ID of the player who uses their ability

arguments.name: string
arguments.description: Classname of ability. Possible values: 'ability_tongue', 'ability_vomit', 'ability_lunge', 'ability_spit', 'ability_charge', 'ability_throw' for the Smoker, Boomer, Hunter, Spitter, Charger and Tank, respectively.

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: enum of the way it was used (different for each ability)

events.name: ammo_pickup
notes: Fires when a survivor fills their primary weapon's ammo by using weapon_ammo_spawn entity.

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: The player who got some ammo from a weapon_ammo_spawner

events.name: item_pickup
notes: Fires when a survivor picks up a weapon.
It does not fire when picking up primary weapons and pistols on non weapon_*_spawn entities, excluding the give command.

arguments.name: string
arguments.description: either a weapon such as 'tmp' or 'hegrenade', or an item such as 'nvgs'

events.name: golden_crowbar_pickup
notes: Fires when a survivor picks up a golden crowbar.

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: The player who picked up the crowbar

events.name: grenade_bounce
notes: Fires when a grenade launcher projectile lands, or a thrown pipe bomb/molotov/bile jar bounces.

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: user ID of the projectile's owner

arguments.name: float
arguments.description: the projectile's position in the x-axis

arguments.name: float
arguments.description: the projectile's position in the y-axis

arguments.name: float
arguments.description: the projectile's position in the z-axis

events.name: hegrenade_detonate
notes: Fires when molotov projectiles, pipe bomb projectiles, propane tanks or oxygen tanks detonate.

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: user ID of the detonated entities' owner

arguments.name: float
arguments.description: detonated entity's position in the x-axis

arguments.name: float
arguments.description: detonated entity's position in the y-axis

arguments.name: float
arguments.description: detonated entity's position in the z-axis

events.name: bullet_impact
notes: Fires on bullet impact.

events.name: player_jump
notes: Fires when a player jumps.

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: user ID of the player who jumps

events.name: player_falldamage
notes: Fires when a player takes fall damage.

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: Who caused them to do so (if anyone)

events.name: player_ledge_grab
notes: Fires when a survivor hangs from a ledge.

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: Who caused them to do so (if anyone)

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: person who released from the ledge

arguments.name: bool
arguments.description: Is the door a checkpoint door

arguments.name: bool
arguments.description: Was the door closed when it started opening?

arguments.name: bool
arguments.description: Is the door a checkpoint door

arguments.name: bool
arguments.description: Is the door a checkpoint door

events.name: waiting_checkpoint_door_used
events.description: Someone tried to open a checkpoint door that is locked till everyone loads in

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: player who tried to open it

events.name: waiting_door_used_versus
events.description: Someone tried to open a checkpoint door that is locked till everyone loads in

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: player who tried to open it

events.name: waiting_checkpoint_button_used
events.description: Someone tried to push a button that's locked until everyone is gathered

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: player who tried to open it

events.name: success_checkpoint_button_used
events.description: Someone pushed a button that's locked until everyone is gathered

events.name: round_start
notes: Related Events: Fires alongside round_start_pre_entity and round_start_post_nav. Event order: round_start_pre_entity, round_start_post_nav, round_start.

events.name: round_start_pre_entity
notes: Related Events: Fires alongside round_start_post_nav and round_start. Event order: round_start_pre_entity, round_start_post_nav, round_start.

events.name: round_start_post_nav
notes: Related Events: Fires alongside round_start_pre_entity and round_start. Event order: round_start_pre_entity, round_start_post_nav, round_start.

arguments.name: long
arguments.description: entity id of the player who initiated the vote

events.name: vote_cast_yes
notes: Fires when a player votes yes on an ongoing vote.

events.name: vote_cast_no
notes: Fires when a player votes no on an ongoing vote.

events.name: infected_hurt
notes: Fires when an infected takes damage. Registers for non-playable classes (Common Infected, Witch). See player_hurt for other playable classes

arguments.description: don't network this, its way too spammy

events.name: infected_death
notes: Fires when an infected dies.

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: ID of the infected that died

arguments.name: bool
arguments.description: signals a death from blast damage

events.name: difficulty_changed
events.description: It appears that strDifficulty is z_difficulty value
notes: Fires when the difficulty is changed ingame.
Alongside a successful vote, it also fires when the difficulty is manually changed via z_difficulty convar.

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: number of survivors that made it out

events.name: mission_lost
events.description: As in, the survivor team failed. Opposite of finale_win, but not necessarily during the finale.

events.name: final_reportscreen
events.description: Right before the final report screen comes up, let the awards possibly fire

events.name: map_transition
events.description: When campaign cinematics start

events.name: player_transitioned
notes: Fires when a player has transitioned to a new chapter from the previous chapter in a campaign. While in coop or realism and the map is not a first session (!Director.IsSessionStartMap()), fires after a map reset because of mission loss. Fires alongside player_spawn, if the survivor has died from the previous chapter, this event fires first, and vice versa. This event never fires on versus; the game treats every versus game as the first session. Fires when switching campaigns via voting, even though Director.IsSessionStartMap() returns true, it is not the same as using the changelevel convar, players transition as they are dead from the previous.

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: the person that just finished transitioning

events.name: heal_begin
notes: Fires when a survivor starts using a medkit.

events.name: heal_success
notes: Fires when a survivor successfully uses a medkit.

events.name: heal_end
events.description: Issues: subject is broken for this event, it always appears to be the player doing the healing

events.name: heal_interrupted
notes: Fires when a survivor's healing process from a medkit is interrupted.

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: person who was being healed, but moved.

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: The ID of the weapon given

events.name: pills_used
notes: Fires when a survivor successfully uses pain pills.

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: person who tried to use the pills

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: person who tried to use the ammo pack

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: person who tried to use the ammo pack

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: person who tried to use the ammo pack

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: person who tried to use an ammo pile with a grenade launcher

events.name: defibrillator_begin
notes: Fires when a survivor starts using a defibrillator.

events.name: defibrillator_used
notes: Fires when a survivor successfully uses a defibrillator.

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: person who tried to use the defibrillator

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: person who was defibrillating, but moved.

events.name: upgrade_pack_begin
notes: Fires when a survivor starts deploying an upgrade pack.

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: person who is deploying the pack

events.name: upgrade_pack_used
notes: Fires when a survivor successfully deploys an upgrade pack.

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: entity index of the deployed upgrade pack (either upgrade_ammo_explosive or upgrade_ammo_incendiary )

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: person who is deploying the pack

events.name: upgrade_item_already_used
notes: Fires when a survivor tries to use an already used upgrade pack.

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: person who tried to use an ammo upgrade twice

arguments.name: string
arguments.description: classname of the upgrade we tried to use

events.name: upgrade_failed_no_primary
notes: Fires when a survivor tries to use an unused upgrade pack with no primary weapon.
If the upgrade pack has been used and the survivor has no primary weapon, upgrade_item_already_used fires instead of this event.

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: person who tried to use an upgrade without having a primary weapon

arguments.name: string
arguments.description: name of the upgrade we tried to use, eg "INCENDIARY_AMMO"

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: The player who sees the entity

arguments.name: long
arguments.description: Entindex of the entity they see

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: user id of the dead player represented

events.name: adrenaline_used
notes: Fires when a survivor successfully uses an adrenaline.

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: person who had and used the adrenaline

events.name: revive_begin
notes: Fires when a survivor starts reviving an incapacitated survivor.

events.name: revive_success
notes: Fires when a survivor successfully revives an incapacitated survivor.

arguments.name: bool
arguments.description: person revived dies if they fall again

arguments.name: bool
arguments.description: 1 if person revived was ledge hanging

events.name: player_incapacitated
notes: Fires when a survivor becomes incapacitated, or a tank is killed.

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: user ID who made us incapacitated

arguments.name: long
arguments.description: if attacker not player, entindex of who made us incapacitated

events.name: player_incapacitated_start
notes: Fires when a survivor is about to become incapacitated.
Use this event to check a survivor's state before becoming incapacitated.

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: user ID who made us incapacitated

arguments.name: long
arguments.description: if attacker not player, entindex of who made us incapacitated

events.name: player_entered_start_area
events.description: When a player spawns into the player start area

events.name: player_first_spawn
notes: Fires when a player entity is spawned for the first time.
When a player idles, a survivor bot is spawned, firing this event.

events.name: player_left_start_area
events.description: When a player leaves the player start area

events.name: player_left_safe_area
events.description: When a player leaves the safe area

events.name: player_entered_checkpoint
notes: Fires when a player entity enters a checkpoint area.

arguments.name: long
arguments.description: If not a player, the entity index of the one entering

arguments.name: long
arguments.description: Entity index of the checkpoint door the player entered to get here.

arguments.name: string
arguments.description: name of the entity they see

events.name: player_left_checkpoint
notes: Fires when a player entity leavess a checkpoint area.

arguments.name: long
arguments.description: If not a player, the entity index of the one exiting

events.name: player_shoved
notes: Fires when a survivor shoves another survivor. Issues: Does not fire when shoving a survivor that is currently in combat.

events.name: entity_shoved
notes: Fires when a survivor shoves a non-point entity, except survivors.

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: the entity index of the one who was shoved

events.name: player_jump_apex
notes: Fires when a player reaches the highest point of their jump. In other words, their upward velocity becomes 0, right before the player starts falling.
Using noclip fires this event constantly.

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: player index who was trying to move

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: player index who kept them from moving

events.name: player_now_it
notes: Fires when a player entity is covered in bile.

arguments.name: bool
arguments.description: whether it was vomit or explosion

events.name: player_no_longer_it
notes: Fires when a player entity's bile effect wears off.

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: Player who is now no longer it

events.name: witch_harasser_set
notes: Fires when a witch is startled.
Using CommandABot on the witch also fires this event.

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: Player who woke up the witch

arguments.name: bool
arguments.description: First time the witch set a harasser

events.name: witch_spawn
notes: Fires when a witch is spawned.

arguments.name: long
arguments.description: Entindex of witch spawning right now.

events.name: witch_killed
notes: Fires when a witch dies.

arguments.name: long
arguments.description: Entindex of witch that was killed.

arguments.name: bool
arguments.description: TRUE if the Witch was killed with one shot

arguments.name: bool
arguments.description: TRUE if the witch was only killed by melee attacks (no blast, burn, or bullet damage)

events.name: tank_spawn
notes: Fires when a witch spawns.

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: User ID of the tank spawning now

arguments.name: long
arguments.description: Entindex of tank spawning right now.

arguments.name: long
arguments.description: Entindex of infected what got killed

arguments.name: long
arguments.description: entity id of other party in the award, if any

events.name: tongue_grab
notes: Fires when a smoker starts grabbing a survivor.

events.name: tongue_broke_bent
notes: Fires when a smoker's tongue breaks due to world geometry bend.

events.name: tongue_release
notes: Fires when a smoker releases a survivor, whether choked or not, etc.
Fires twice in all release cases, except tongue_broke_bent where it only fires once. Second iteration returns distance and victim arguments as 0.

events.name: choke_start
notes: Fires when a smoker starts choking a survivor.

events.name: choke_end
notes: Fires when a smoker stops choking a survivor, regardless of the situation.

events.name: choke_stopped
events.description: Issues: While the event does not fire while staggering the smoker with the methods above, it fires when it is killed using only the grenade launcher and explosive ammo.
notes: Fires when a smoker tongue is cleared on a choked player, fires alongside choke_end (choke_stopped fires first).

Does not fire when (choke_end still fires): The smoker is staggered with a propane/oxygen tank, pipe bomb, explosive ammo, grenade launcher, boomer explosion, and charger impact. Another special takes over the pinned survivor or kills the smoker. The smoker is killed by a gas can, a firework box, a Molotov, and incendiary ammo. The smoker ledge-hanged the survivor. Using Stagger() on either the smoker or the survivor.

events.name: tongue_pull_stopped
events.description: Issues: While the event does not fire while staggering the smoker with the methods above, it fires when it is killed using only the grenade launcher and explosive ammo.
notes: Fires when a smoker tongue is cleared on a dragging player. Includes cuts.

Does not fire when: The smoker is staggered with a propane/oxygen tank, pipe bomb, explosive ammo, grenade launcher, boomer explosion, and charger impact. Another special takes over the pinned survivor or kills the smoker. The smoker is killed by a gas can, a firework box, a Molotov, and incendiary ammo. The smoker ledge-hanged the survivor. Using Stagger() on either the smoker or the survivor.

events.name: lunge_pounce
notes: Fires when a hunter successfully pounces a survivor.

arguments.name: long
arguments.description: Distance from pounce start to contact

events.name: pounce_stopped
events.description: Issues: Shoving a hunter right after it pounces a survivor does not release the survivor, but the event still fires.
notes: Fires whenever a player clears a hunter dominating a survivor, fires alongside pounce_end (pounce_stopped is fired first).

Does not fire if staggered by pipe bomb, propane/oxygen tank, explosive ammo, grenade launcher, boomer explosion, charger impact, and Stagger(). (pounce_end still fires) Fires if the hunter is killed by any method above, except boomer explosion and charger impact. A special that kills the hunter returns userid as the special's userid. Killing the hunter by shoving fires this event before player_hurt. However, killing it by any other means fires the event after player_hurt.

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: And who was killed or incapped

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: The actual player entity who is awaiting rescue.

arguments.name: long
arguments.description: SurvivorRescue entity representing the player who needs to be rescued from the closet (used for position)

events.name: survivor_rescued
notes: Fires when a survivor is successfully rescued from a rescue closet.

arguments.name: long
arguments.description: ent index of the rescue door

events.name: weapon_drop

events.name: player_use
notes: Fires when a Survivor presses +USE on a useable entity. i.e. Weapons, items, doors. Related Events: If targetid is an item, item_pickup is called prior to player_use
It does not fire when using a mounted gun. It does not fire when the Use input is fired on a survivor.

arguments.name: short

events.name: weapon_pickup
events.description: client event for player has picked up a weapon. If that does not work, try using the "item_pickup" event.

arguments.description: gender of the Infected that dropped the weapon

arguments.name: bool
arguments.description: TRUE if the Hunter was in the act of lunging

arguments.name: bool
arguments.description: TRUE if the Hunter was in the act of lunging

arguments.name: bool
arguments.description: true if incendiary ammo was used

events.name: boomer_exploded
notes: Fires when a boomer explodes. Related Events: Fires after player_death

arguments.name: bool
arguments.description: Exploding boomer splashed bile on Survivors

events.name: non_pistol_fired
notes: Fires when a survivor attacks with a non-pistol weapon.

Only fires once for each survivor; subsequent non-pistol weapons being shot do not fire this event. Most likely used to detect if a survivor has used a melee weapon or not for melee-only achievements. It continues firing again after a map transition, reset and team swap.

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: User that fired a non-pistol weapon

events.name: weapon_fire_at_40
events.description: This is networked, special event for game instructor

events.name: total_ammo_below_40
events.description: sent for any ammo type, except those with max ammo 1, or infinite ammo, like pistols

events.name: player_hurt_concise
events.description: Abbreviated version of 'player_hurt' that is networked

arguments.name: long
arguments.description: entity id who attacked, if attacker not a player, and userid therefore invalid

events.name: tank_killed
notes: Fires when a tank dies. Issues: It does not fire if the tank is not a bot.

arguments.name: bool
arguments.description: TRUE if a player single-handedly killed the Tank

arguments.name: bool
arguments.description: TRUE if the tank was only killed by melee attacks (no blast, burn, or bullet damage)

arguments.name: bool
arguments.description: TRUE if l4d1 survivors inflicted damage and the l4d2 survivors did not)

events.name: tank_rock_killed
notes: Fires when a survivor destroys a tank rock.

events.name: achievement_write_failed
events.description: Used for a notification message when an achievement fails to write

events.name: ghost_spawn_time
events.description: Used for clients to know how long until they become a ghost (and can spawn)

arguments.name: short

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: How long of a wait until player is a ghost

events.name: survival_at_30min
notes: Fires when the survivors survive 30 minutes on a survival map. Used to know when we elapse 30 minutes on a survival map

events.name: survival_at_10min
notes: Fires when the survivors survive 10 minutes on a survival map. Used to know when we elapse 10 minutes on a survival map

events.name: explain_pre_drawbridge
events.description: Point out the button that starts the gauntlet finale.

events.name: receive_upgrade
notes: Fires when a survivor successfully receives an incendiary, explosive, or laser sight upgrades.

events.name: spit_burst
notes: Fires when a spitter's spit hits the ground.
Spitter's spit on death also fires this event.

events.name: charger_killed
notes: Fires on a charger's death.

arguments.name: bool
arguments.description: TRUE if a player killed the charger with a melee weapon

arguments.name: bool
arguments.description: TRUE if the charger was charging when it died

events.name: spitter_killed
notes: Fires on a spitter's death.

arguments.name: bool
arguments.description: TRUE if the spitter spit at some point

events.name: jockey_ride
notes: Fires when a jockey successfully rides a survivor.

events.name: jockey_ride_end
notes: Fires when a jockey releases a survivor.

rescuer return 0 when: The jockey is being cleared by shoving. Another special takes over the jockeyed survivor. The commons kill the biled jockey. The jockey incapacitates the survivor, including ledge-hangs. The jockey is killed or stumbled by a charger's impact.

A jockey stumbled by a boomer explosion returns rescuer argument as the boomer's userid. Using Stagger() on the jockey returns rescuer argument as the jockey's userid. Using Stagger() on the survivor returns rescuer argument as the survivor's userid.

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: user ID of player that shoved the jockey

arguments.name: bool
arguments.description: TRUE if Jockey was in the act of lunging

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: user ID of player that landed a headshot on the Jockey

arguments.name: bool
arguments.description: TRUE if Jockey was in the act of lunging

events.name: jockey_killed
notes: Fires on a jockey's death.

events.name: non_melee_fired
notes: Fires when a survivor attacks with a non-melee weapon.

Only fires once for each survivor; subsequent non-melee weapons being fired do not fire this event. Most likely used to detect if a survivor has used a non-melee weapon or not for non-melee achievements. It continues firing again after a map transition, reset and team swap.

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: User that fired a non-melee weapon

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: userid of the player who did the decapitation

events.name: vomit_bomb_tank
notes: Fires when a survivor biled a tank using a vomit jar.
Does not fire when biled using HitWithVomit() script function.

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: userid of the player who biled the tank

events.name: triggered_car_alarm
notes: Fires when a survivor triggers a car alarm.

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: user ID of player who activated car alarm

events.name: charger_charge_start
notes: Fires when a charger starts charging. Issues: Called more than once per ability use. For consistency, check ability_use Issues: Can be called after charger_charge_end. Use ability_use for an alternative start-to-end relationship.

events.name: charger_charge_end
notes: Fires when a charger stops charging.

events.name: charger_carry_start
notes: Fires when a charger starts carrying a survivor while charging.

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: user ID of the player who was charged

events.name: charger_carry_end
notes: Fires when a charger stops charging while carrying a survivor.

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: user ID of the player who was charged

events.name: charger_carry_kill
notes: Fires when a survivor dies while being carried by a charger.
Only works on competitive gamemodes, like versus.

events.name: charger_impact
notes: Fires when a survivor is sent flying by a charger’s charge impact.

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: user ID of the player who was impacted

events.name: charger_pummel_start
notes: Fires when a charger starts pummeling a survivor.

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: user ID of the player who was charged

events.name: charger_pummel_end
notes: Fires when a charger stops pummeling a survivor.

Killing the charger or survivor being pummeled to death returns rescuer as 0. It only returns rescuer as the survivor's userid when the charger is staggered by a pipe bomb, explosive ammo, propane/oxygen tank. Using Stagger() on the survivor returns rescuer argument as 0. Using Stagger() on the charger returns rescuer argument as the charger's userid.

events.name: strongman_bell_knocked_off
events.description: The arcade bell on c2m4_barns

events.name: survival_round_start
notes: Fires when a survivor round starts.

events.name: m60_streak_ended
events.description: I was holding down the m60 trigger, and now I'm not

events.name: stashwhacker_game_won
notes: Fires after winning a game of stashwhacker in chapter 4 of Dark Carnival (c2m4).

arguments.name: long
arguments.description: entity index of the killed entity

arguments.name: string
arguments.description: The IP address and source port of the client for the connection

arguments.name: short
arguments.description: Flag indicating whether the client is a bot or player
